data.title: Flex to Acquire Electrical Power Products for About $1.1 Billion
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Flex agreed to acquire Electrical Power Products in an all-cash transaction valued at roughly $1.1 billion, including about $100 million in anticipated tax benefits.

Chief Executive Officer Revathi Advaithi said Monday that the deal will expand Flex's critical power capabilities, allowing the company to play a larger role in modernizing the U.S. electrical infrastructure.

The acquisition is expected to be accretive to Flex's adjusted per-share earnings in the first full year after closing, anticipated to occur in the first quarter of Flex's fiscal 2027.

Electrical Power Products, based in Des Moines, Iowa, designs and manufactures engineered-to-order electrical power control and protection systems for utility, power generation, and industrial clients. The company is expected to generate revenue of about $323 million in its fiscal year ending March 31, with double-digit organic growth projected.

President Tim O'Donnell said joining Flex will support the company's long-term growth while maintaining its engineering-driven culture.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Aluminum Is the New Oil. These Stocks Are Soaring. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

Alcoa, Century Aluminum and other mining stocks were jumping early Monday, as two major Middle Eastern aluminum producers sustained damage to their production facilities over the weekend.

Alcoa was up 8.1% to $63.12 in premarket trading. Heading into Monday's trading, the stock is down 5.9% over the past month since the Iran began. During that time, the S&P 500 index has dropped 7.4%.

Century Aluminum, down around 4% since the conflict started, pointed 8.6% higher ahead of the open. Constellium and Kaiser Aluminum were both around 2% higher.

As the war in Iran began, Alcoa shares fell alongside the broader market amid fears of slowing consumer and industrial demand linked to higher energy prices.

But it's recent rebound may have been driven by supply constrains. State-backed giants Emirates Global Aluminium and Aluminium Bahrain were attacked on Saturday, The Wall Street Journal reported.

The Middle East accounts for 9% of global aluminum production according to ANZ. In turn, the bank says four-to-five million metric tons of exports are at risk.

Indeed, aluminum forward contracts were up 4.8%, at $3,342 per metric ton, in New York early Monday, according to FactSet data. Prices are now up 10% since Feb. 27, the day before fighting began.

Looking ahead, Alcoa could benefit from stronger margins and renewed investor interest linked to supply chokeholds — turning what began as a broad selloff into a potential tailwind for the stock.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: China Zheshang Bank: Net profit declined 14.85% to RMB12.93 billion, but asset quality and capital ratios remained robust

data.title: Genco Says Diana Offer Undervalues Company, Flags Proxy Fight Risk
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Genco Shipping & Trading on Monday urged shareholders to reject an unsolicited takeover attempt by rival Diana Shipping, arguing the proposal undervalues the company and warning that a related proxy fight could put investor returns at risk.

Geneco said in a letter to shareholders that its board reviewed Diana's $23.50-a-share offer earlier this month before determining that it "fails to provide shareholders with an appropriate premium."

The dry bulk shipper also cautioned that Diana's effort to replace Genco's entire board is not a vote on the acquisition itself, but rather on whether to cede control of the company to Diana's nominees. Genco said such a move could lead to a transaction at less favorable terms or strategic changes that could undermine its business model.

The letter marks Genco's latest response in an escalating battle with Diana, which earlier this month said it would proceed with a proxy contest after Genco rejected a sweetened bid for the company. Diana Chief Executive Semiramis Paliou has previously accused Genco's leadership of prioritizing entrenchment over shareholder value, while Genco has countered that the offer fails to capture its improving financial profile and dividend-generating capacity.

Diana, which owns about 15% of Genco, has sought to replace the company's board after its acquisition proposals were rebuffed. Genco has said it remains open to engaging on a transaction that properly reflects its value.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Dollar Could Rise Further in Near Term Before Turning Lower — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar could rise further in the near term due to the energy price shock stemming from the Iran war, Morgan Stanley strategists say in a note. However, once markets shift away from pricing the inflationary aspects of higher energy prices and focus on the negative growth implications, the dollar could turn lower as defense spending rises, they say. They advise investors to sell the currency on any rallies rather than buying it on any declines. The DXY dollar index rises 0.1% to 100.293 and Morgan Stanley expects it to see a final rally to 102 before turning lower. America's position as a net oil exporter is helping the dollar. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Perseus Operating Group of Constellation Software Completes Acquisition of IronHQ
data.description:
(13:23 GMT) *Perseus Operating Group Of Constellation Software Completes Acquisition Of IronHQ >CSU.T
(13:23 GMT) Press Release: Perseus Operating Group of Constellation Software Completes Acquisition of IronHQ

TORONTO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 30, 2026--

Perseus Operating Group ("Perseus") of Constellation Software Inc. ("Constellation") today announced that it has completed the purchase of IronHQ, a quoting, inventory management, and CRM solution serving the agriculture and construction equipment dealership market.

The acquisition expands Perseus' presence in the North American equipment dealership software market and represents its 11th acquisition within the dealer ecosystem under the Dealership portfolio (https://constellationdealer.com). IronHQ will join the Dealer Information Systems (DIS) business (https://www.discorp.com), where its complementary capabilities will strengthen Perseus' dealership software offering and support continued growth for equipment dealers across North America.

"We are pleased to welcome the IronHQ team and customers to Perseus and the Constellation Dealer Group," said Sean Raynor, President of Constellation Dealer Group. "IronHQ is a strong strategic fit within our dealership portfolio and meaningfully enhances our end--to--end offering for equipment dealers. Bringing IronHQ into the DIS business further strengthens a portfolio with a proven market position while expanding the functionality available to our customers."

"On behalf of the DIS team, we are excited to welcome IronHQ into the organization," said Shane Waldemar, GM of DIS. "IronHQ's quoting, inventory, and CRM capabilities are highly complementary to DIS, and together we will be able to deliver even greater value to our dealers. This addition reinforces our commitment to investing in solutions that help equipment dealers operate more efficiently, serve their customers better, and grow their businesses."

About Perseus

Perseus is an operating group of Constellation Software Inc. Perseus acquires and invests in software businesses that are committed to providing long-term solutions and life-long partnerships to customers. For more information about Perseus, visit: https://csiperseus.com/

About Constellation Software Inc.

Constellation Software's common shares are listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol "CSU". Constellation Software acquires, manages and builds vertical market software businesses. Further information about Constellation may be obtained from its website at www.csisoftware.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260323075607/en/

CONTACT: For More Information:

Nikki Jefferson

Senior Director, Marketing

nikki.jefferson@csiperseus.com
